compiled_state.title: As a developer, RED test failures are triaged by an opus evaluator
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Description

**What**: Create the `dso:red-test-evaluator` plugin agent definition at `plugins/dso/agents/red-test-evaluator.md`. This opus-tier agent receives RED test writer failure messages and returns a structured verdict: REVISE (with impact assessment of in-progress and closed tasks), REJECT (retry at opus tier), or CONFIRM (TDD infeasible for this task type).

**Why**: When the RED test writer cannot produce a behavioral test, the system needs intelligent triage — not silent failure. The evaluator determines whether the task spec needs revision, the writer needs a stronger model, or TDD is genuinely infeasible for this type of work.

**Scope**:
- IN: Agent definition file, REVISE/REJECT/CONFIRM verdict schema (JSON), impact assessment logic for REVISE, CONFIRM categories (infrastructure, injection, documentation, reference removal)
- OUT: Orchestrator integration (Stories 3, 4), REVISE count tracking (orchestrator responsibility)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, a `dso:red-test-evaluator` agent definition exists at `plugins/dso/agents/red-test-evaluator.md` with model tier opus
  ← Satisfies: "A dso:red-test-evaluator plugin agent (opus)"
- When this story is complete, the agent returns one of REVISE, REJECT, or CONFIRM as a structured JSON verdict when given a failure message + task/story/epic context
  ← Satisfies: "returns a structured verdict: REVISE, REJECT, or CONFIRM"
- When this story is complete, REVISE verdicts include an impact assessment identifying which in-progress and closed tasks are affected by the revision
  ← Satisfies: "REVISE (with impact assessment of in-progress and closed tasks)"
- When this story is complete, CONFIRM verdicts specify the infeasibility category (infrastructure, injection, documentation, or reference removal)
  ← Satisfies: "CONFIRM (TDD infeasible)"
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Testing] Need behavioral tests for each verdict branch — representative failure inputs that trigger REVISE, REJECT, and CONFIRM
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
